task_completion.description: At least two distinct simulated device adapters are started in the OpenICE Supervisor
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenICE Supervisor GUI running with at least two different simulated devices (e.g., Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter) active.

task_completion.description: Bash script event_monitor.sh is created to extract and count events
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/event_monitor.sh is visible in a text editor or terminal, containing bash commands (e.g., grep, awk, sort, uniq) designed to parse /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log.

task_completion.description: Event summary is saved to event_summary.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/event_summary.txt is visible (via cat or text editor) and contains extracted device-related events along with their occurrence counts.

task_completion.description: Log entry structure is documented in log_format_doc.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/log_format_doc.txt is visible and includes a description of the log format (e.g., timestamp, level, logger, message).

task_completion.description: Three observed log patterns are documented in log_format_doc.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/log_format_doc.txt explicitly lists at least three distinct log patterns observed from the OpenICE logs.

task_completion.description: Clinical event monitoring recommendations are documented in log_format_doc.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/Desktop/log_format_doc.txt contains a section with recommendations for monitoring clinical events based on the logs.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenICE Supervisor GUI to generate real logs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual OpenICE Supervisor graphical interface being used to start devices, rather than the agent manually creating or faking the openice.log file.

integrity.description: The bash script performs actual log parsing
integrity.visual_evidence: The event_monitor.sh script uses standard text processing tools (grep, awk, etc.) to process the log file, rather than just echoing hardcoded text into the summary file.

integrity.description: The simulated devices started are distinct
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor GUI shows at least two different types of simulated devices or distinct device instances, not just a single device.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenICE 2.0 log files are generated at /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log in standard Log4j format (timestamp, level, logger, message). The agent must start at least 2 distinct simulated device adapters in the OpenICE Supervisor GUI to generate synthetic vital signs data. The bash script must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/event_monitor.sh, the analysis output at /home/ga/Desktop/event_summary.txt, and the documentation at /home/ga/Desktop/log_format_doc.txt.
